title: Adopt tree-sitter bash parser for command validation (parity with CC utils/bash/bashParser.ts)
description:
## Gap

Claude Code uses a tree-sitter AST-based bash parser (utils/bash/bashParser.ts ~4436 lines, utils/bash/treeSitterAnalysis.ts ~506 lines) for command validation and path analysis. The AST path is the primary (non-deprecated) validation route:
- checkPathConstraints (pathValidation.ts:1013) uses AST-derived SimpleCommand[] when available
- validateSinglePathCommandArgv (pathValidation.ts:888) operates on pre-parsed argv from AST
- checkReadOnlyConstraints (readOnlyValidation.ts:1876) calls tryParseShellCommand which uses tree-sitter
- AST avoids the shell-quote single-quote backslash bug that affects the deprecated string-parsing path

OpenClaudia has no tree-sitter parser. Command validation is pure substring/regex denylist (src/tools/bash/policy.rs:89-133). No AST analysis of command structure is performed.

## Parity reference
- CC: utils/bash/bashParser.ts (AST parse), utils/bash/treeSitterAnalysis.ts (analysis helpers), pathValidation.ts (AST-backed path checking)
- OC: missing entirely

## Acceptance
- tree-sitter-bash crate integration (or equivalent Rust parser)
- SimpleCommand / argv extraction from parsed AST
- AST-backed path validation replacing or augmenting the current regex denylist
- Handles quoted strings, heredocs, compound commands correctly
